landing.headline:
German public discourse —
Made visible.
landing.description: Two dimensions. Nineteen media outlets. Six parties. Twenty years of manifestos. Where does Germany converge — and where does it divide?
landing.dim1_subtitle: How do 19 German media outlets cover societal debates?
landing.dim1_body: Updated daily. Eight political topics. From left to right — where is there consensus, where does discourse begin?
landing.dim2_subtitle: How have party positions evolved from 2005 to 2025?
landing.dim2_body: Semantic analysis of all Bundestag election manifestos. Who converges, who diverges — and what do election results say about it?

home.headline:
How does Germany report
on itself?
home.description: 19 German media outlets. 8 political topics. Updated daily. Where is there consensus — and where does discourse begin?
home.media_intro: 19 German media outlets — analysed since March 2026

topicview.snippet_hint: A selection of article headlines per political leaning.
topicview.pending_desc: This topic will be analysed in the next daily ML run.

partyview.headline: How have party positions evolved?
partyview.subtitle: 6 federal elections · 6 parties · 8 topics · Who are the bridge builders?
partyview.network_desc: The network shows the content proximity between party manifestos for the {{year}} federal election. Thicker edges indicate higher programmatic overlap. Node size reflects a party's bridging score.
partyview.error: Analysis unavailable — is the backend reachable?
partyview.bridging_text: The bridge builder score measures how close a party is programmatically to the centre of the entire party spectrum. Technically, it is the normalised mean of cosine similarities of a party to all others — 0 means maximum isolation, 1 means maximum proximity to the field. Normalisation is applied across all election years so that values remain comparable.
partyview.bridging_findings: CDU/CSU and SPD historically occupy the central positions — as catch-all parties they write broad policy packages that overlap with many others. The AfD consistently shows the lowest score, reflecting its programmatic isolation. The Greens and FDP vary more, depending on the thematic focus of each campaign.

frag_nach.subheadline: Ask your question. The manifestos answer.

analytics.headline: Diskursraum Analytics

analytics.emotion_info: The model classifies texts into 28 emotion categories based on the GoEmotions dataset. The neutral class is hidden.
